item.title: Soaring Profits in EM Build the Case for a Raging Bull Market
item.description: For the first time in four years, companies in emerging markets are beating profit estimates, giving investors a fresh reason to believe the bull market is just getting started.

item.title: The ‘Mass Affluent’ Are Losing Their Allure for Wealth Managers Navigating AI
item.description: Wealth managers keen to stay relevant in the age of artificial intelligence may soon find that clients with a mere $1 million in liquid assets are no longer worth spending human hours on.

item.title: Aluminum’s War Shock Blunted by Dark Transits and Chinese Supply
item.description: The Iran war caused one of the biggest supply shocks to ever hit the aluminum market, but the runaway price surge that many were bracing for has been blunted by the ingenuity of producers from the Middle East to China.

item.title: Three Indian Tankers Reemerge, Pointing to Hormuz Traffic Uptick
item.description: Three fully laden India-linked supertankers have re-emerged in the Gulf of Oman, adding to increased observed bi-directional traffic across the northern and southern routes of the Strait of Hormuz even as conflicting narratives over the status of transits persist.

item.title: India Seeks US Tariff Edge Over Rivals Before Enacting Trade Pact
item.description: India is seeking a competitive advantage over rival nations before rolling out a much-delayed trade agreement with the US, after both countries agreed on the initial framework.

item.title: Swiss Voters Set to Reject Stricter Neutrality, Early Poll Shows
item.description: Swiss voters are set to reject an initiative to strengthen neutrality, according to an early poll, a result that could support keeping up sanctions against Russia.

item.title: A Stock Trader’s Guide to Navigating a Rare ‘Super El Niño’
item.description: As concerns over the Iran war recede, stock investors are confronting another threat: climate risk, which is prompting a reassessment of bets across sectors from agriculture to insurance.

item.title: Fed’s Favorite Gauge Is Seen Showing Faster Inflation
item.description: The latest update to the Federal Reserve’s favorite inflation gauge is unlikely to challenge a growing consensus at the US central bank around the need for interest-rate hikes this year.

item.title: Millions in Brazil Get Fake Government Mobile Alert After Hack
item.description: Brazil’s Integration and Regional Development Ministry and federal police are investigating an intrusion into the civil defense alert system that sent messages containing the word “misanthropy” to the mobile phones of millions of Brazilians.

item.title: Trump’s Fears About Economy Undercuts US Leverage in Iran Talks
item.description: President Donald Trump said the prospect of global economic collapse was a big reason he signed an interim peace deal with Iran. That admission exposes a key US weakness heading into the next round of talks with Tehran.

item.title: Brazil’s Lula Holds Lead Against Bolsonaro After Bank Scandal
item.description: President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva maintained his lead in polls on the Brazilian presidential election against Senator Flavio Bolsonaro, who is weighed down by his ties to a former banker involved in the country’s biggest bank fraud scandal.

item.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Warns Inflation Expectations Are Rising
item.description: South Africa’s central bank governor said policymakers are seeing early signs of second-round inflation effects as underlying price pressures build, emphasizing the need to act.

item.title: Fed Governor Bowman Spoke at Private Dinner After Policy Meeting
item.description: Michelle Bowman, the Federal Reserve’s vice chair for supervision, spoke at a private dinner in New York hours after attending the central bank’s policy meeting, the Wall Street Journal reported, a period when officials are restricted from commenting on monetary policy.

item.title: Iraq Tells Oil Fields to Start Lifting Output After US-Iran Deal
item.description: Iraq asked operators of five major oil fields to boost production to prewar levels, targeting output of over 3 million barrels a day, after US-Iran deal that aims to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Jio Platforms Plans $3 Billion Debt Reduction from IPO Proceeds
item.description: Jio Platforms Ltd. plans to use part of the proceeds from its proposed initial share sale to repay its telecom unit’s external commercial borrowings of almost $3 billion, a draft IPO document showed.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Warsh’s Tenure at the Fed Begins
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh presided over his first policy meeting since taking the helm of the US central bank, vowing to restore price stability as officials signaled growing support for interest-rate hikes this year.

item.title: How Substack Creators Are Covering This Strange Markets Era
item.description: A panel with James van Geelen, Sam Ro, and Jasmine Sun.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Substack Creators Are Thinking About AI (Podcast)
item.description: We closed out our New York live show on May 28 with a panel that featured three of our favorite Substackers: James van Geelen of Citrini Research, Sam Ro, founder of The TKer, and journalist Jasmine Sun. They’ve all been Odd Lots guests before, and we wanted to get them together to discuss how journalists and analysts are supposed to cover this incredibly strange and highly pressurized moment in markets. Not only has AI basically infected every corner of the world, the media included, but there’

item.title: BYD Rejects Claims It Violated Hungary’s Environmental Rules
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD Co. said it rejected allegations it breached environmental rules in Hungary during the construction of its first plant in the European Union.

item.title: Turkey Gets EU Stance on Russia-Proofing Gas Supply, Reiche Says
item.description: German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche said Turkey understands the European Union’s position that gas imported under new contracts with the bloc must not originate in Russia.

item.title: Europe’s Stocks Are Back in the Lead as Stagflation Risks Ease
item.description: The prospect of peace in the Middle East is setting up European stocks for a standout second half, as investors bet on stronger economic growth and easing inflation.

item.title: Bolivia’s Paz Declares State of Emergency Over Blockades
item.description: Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency following 50 days of road blockades and unrest that have severely disrupted the economy.

item.title: Burnham May Yet Rewrite UK Fiscal Playbook If He Becomes Premier
item.description: After Andy Burnham took a giant leap toward becoming the UK’s next prime minister, focus is turning to how the team he’s assembling could shape the country’s economic policies.

item.title: China’s Key Minerals Exports to Japan Stay at Low Levels in May
item.description: China’s exports of some critical minerals to Japan remained at low levels in May, extending a downturn amid a political dispute between the two countries.

item.title: Germany’s Weirdest Debt Market May No Longer Be Safe Haven for Lenders
item.description: After storied Austrian motorcycle manufacturer KTM AG presented an insolvency plan to creditors last year, several of them convened a video conference to discuss a counter-proposal. More than 100 parties joined, but none of the usual big corporate lenders were involved. Instead, the group featured a motley crew of backers from tiny German towns, Chinese banks and European pension funds, all with wildly varying knowledge of the case at hand. The call was raucous, according to people briefed on th

item.title: Ships Can Use Hormuz Southern Route With Signals On, JMIC Says
item.description: Ships can consider transiting the Strait of Hormuz along the southern route, day or night, with their transponder signals on, a key naval information group has said.

item.title: Deadly H5 Bird Flu Spreads Worldwide With Australia Outbreak
item.description: Australia reported its first mainland case of H5 bird flu, marking the spread of the deadly virus to every continent on the planet.

item.title: Iran War Gives Pakistan Its Biggest Diplomatic Boost in Decades
item.description: Throughout the nearly four-month Iran war, few countries were more visibly at the center of the efforts to end it than Pakistan, an unlikely actor thrust into the spotlight by US President Donald Trump.

item.title: 米イラン和平合意、日銀1％に利上げ、東証終値で初の7万円台に─1週間のニュース5選
item.description: 国際政治もマーケットも新たな局面へ踏み出した1週間。五つのニュースを厳選して振り返ります。

item.title: Trump Tells Axios He Doesn’t See Anthropic as US Security Threat
item.description: President Donald Trump said he doesn’t view Anthropic PBC as a national-security threat, days after his administration took steps to cut off foreign access to the tech company’s most advanced artificial intelligence models.
